key_insight: The paper proposes a fully model-free deep learning framework that uses zeroth-order derivative estimators from simulations to learn solutions and first/second derivatives of high-dimensional fully nonlinear parabolic PDEs with unknown coefficients.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable PDE solving in black-box scientific machine learning and continuous-time reinforcement learning settings where automatic differentiation or explicit system dynamics are impractical.
method: Represent the PDE through two simulator-based data-generating mechanisms and learn solution, gradient, and Hessian networks jointly.
Use zeroth-order derivative estimators from perturbed Monte Carlo trajectories to create training targets from function evaluations only.
Provide a non-asymptotic error analysis decomposing total error into discretization, approximation, statistical, and ZOD bias terms.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments showing competitive performance in moderate and high dimensions, alongside a sample-complexity and Sobolev-space error analysis.
watch_item: ZOD estimators introduce bias–variance tradeoffs and the method still relies on simulator quality, contraction assumptions, and careful handling of discretization error.
paper_title: A Zeroth-Order Deep Learning Method for Fully Nonlinear Parabolic Partial Differential Equations with Unknown Coefficients
